articles.title: 1 dead, 2 others in hospital after late-night shooting in North York
articles.summary: One person is dead after a shooting in North York late Friday night, Toronto police say.

articles.title: Health officials in Peel warn of potential measles exposure at Pearson airport, 2 flights
articles.summary: Public health officials in Peel Region are making the public aware that they may have been exposed to a confirmed case of measles on two flights and at Pearson airport. Peel Public Health says anyone who was on Flight BG305 from Bangladesh to Toronto, which went through Rome, and Flight AC1986 from Toronto to Moncton   on July […]

articles.title: Multiple New York Times reporters issued subpoenas over Air Force One reporting
articles.summary: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration has subpoenaed several New York Times journalists after their report on security concerns involving the new Air Force One, according to the paper. The new jet, which President Donald Trump received as a gift from Qatar, entered service last week. The subpoenas issued Friday seek to force the reporters to testify […]

articles.title: Parking rules next to Toronto park didn't consider accessible permit holders: park volunteer
articles.summary: A slight change to parking rules on a small stub of street adjacent to Dufferin Grove Park has led to a large controversy over accessible parking permit holders.

articles.title: 1 person dead, 2 others seriously injured in North York shooting
articles.summary: One person is dead and two others were seriously injured following a shooting in North York late Friday night. Emergency crews were called to the area of Jane Street and Sheppard Avenue just after 11 p.m.  When officers arrived at the scene, they located two people with gunshot wounds. Both were transported to a hospital […]

articles.title: Pedestrian seriously injured in Mississauga’s Port Credit neighbourhood
articles.summary: A pedestrian was seriously injured Friday night after getting hit by a car in Mississauga’s Port Credit neighbourhood. Emergency crews were called to the area of Lakeshore Road and Hurontario Street just after 9:30 p.m. According to investigators, the pedestrian was crossing the road when they were struck by the vehicle Paramedics say one person […]

articles.title: Church Street pedestrianization pilot project getting warm reviews from residents and businesses
articles.summary: It’s been three weeks since a stretch of Toronto’s Village neighbourhood was closed to vehicle traffic and many people using the area say the atmosphere is a welcome change. Two blocks of Church Street between Wellesley and Alexander Streets have been closed to cars and cyclists since June 19 as part of a pilot project […]

articles.title: Possible measles exposure on at Pearson airport and on 2 flights this week: public health agency
articles.summary: Peel Public Health is warning the public of potential measles exposure at Toronto's Pearson airport and on two flights on July 7.

articles.title: Expanding Billy Bishop runway could degrade Toronto harbour water quality, city report says
articles.summary: A new city report says the province's plan to expand the runway at Billy Bishop airport to accommodate jets could reduce water flow in Toronto's Inner Harbour, limiting its ability to flush sewage and pollutants. Recreational activities, biodiversity and even drinking water could be affected, the report says.

articles.title: 2 dead after driver runs red light, striking pedestrians in Scarborough, police say
articles.summary: A 64-year-old man and 61-year-old woman are dead after they were struck by a driver in Scarborough on Friday afternoon, Toronto police say.

articles.title: AI articles are sharing 'inaccurate' information about flight operations, Pearson Airport warns
articles.summary: An airport spokesperson said "bot authors" are putting out "upward of 100 articles a day with false figures, no attempted verification and misleading context that implies system-wide cascades."

articles.title: Man arrested for arson following a fire at North York seniors' housing building
articles.summary: A man has been arrested following an arson investigation by Toronto police after a fire Thursday at a building housing seniors.

articles.title: Ever heard of a chief positivity officer? This Ontario man's job is to ensure optimism is a town priority
articles.summary: From positivity ambassador to chief positivity officer, Jamie Boyle's role in the Town of Newmarket blossomed over the years as he maintained connections with public services and community groups.

articles.title: How an old Toronto apartment building is getting a second life — without moving tenants out
articles.summary: Imagine a giant winter coat on a 59-year-old apartment building — that’s how the team behind a massive retrofit at 444 Logan Avenue describes the project. The goal is to keep the heat in, lower energy bills and cut carbon emissions.

articles.title: 'She was my baby': Family mourns woman killed in Mississauga hit-and-run
articles.summary: The family of a woman killed in a Mississauga hit-and-run crash are remembering her as a "kind soul" who would would help anyone in need.

articles.title: Body found after crews extinguish shed on fire in Brampton, police say
articles.summary: A body was found in a shed in Brampton, Ont., on Thursday after crews put out a fire that burned the structure, police say.
